sound_type: deep rumbling wall movement -- low-frequency sustained rumble with infrasound component, viscous saturated debris in motion, E=8 maximum
ecosystem: Unstable slope -- glacially deposited sediments (till) over clay substrate, valley walls steepened by river undercutting
season: Episodic (associated with heavy precipitation, rain-on-snow events, prolonged saturation)
memorial_context: The Oso landslide (March 22, 2014, Snohomish County, WA) claimed 43 lives -- the deadliest landslide in US history at that time. This entry documents the geological and acoustic mechanisms with full respect for the community and those lost.

geological_mechanism.substrate_structure: Glacial till (unsorted glacially deposited sediment) over lacustrine clay layer (fine-grained sediment deposited in glacially formed lake)
geological_mechanism.failure_mechanism: Clay saturation → shear strength drops below weight of overlying glacial till → failure along clay interface → saturated material flows as viscous fluid
geological_mechanism.key_property: Clay does not drain quickly (fine grain size resists water movement). Sustained heavy precipitation or rain-on-snow saturates clay beyond capacity.
geological_mechanism.regional_prevalence: Geological structure present throughout Puget Lowland and Cascade foothills. Many valley walls have same general structure: glacial sediments over weaker materials, slopes steepened by river erosion.

acoustic_physics.primary_character: Deep, sustained, low-frequency rumbling. Viscous flow of semi-liquid debris produces low-frequency acoustic energy. Felt physically (infrasound component) before perceived as audible.
acoustic_physics.infrasound: Substantial infrasound component (below 20 Hz). Physical sensation felt in chest and body at close range. Animals detecting infrasound may have advance warning.
acoustic_physics.duration: Tens of seconds to several minutes for main flow. Much longer than glacier calving (seconds) or most rockslides (seconds to tens of seconds).
acoustic_physics.fundamental_frequency_hz: 1-100 Hz primary; infrasound below 20 Hz; some higher-frequency component from debris impact and fragmentation
acoustic_physics.distinction_from_rockslide: Rockslide: dry, percussive, accelerating. Mudslide: low-frequency, rumbling, sustained. Different materials, fundamentally different acoustic character.
acoustic_physics.monitoring_value: Distinctive low-frequency + infrasound signature is detectable by sensor networks. Basis for acoustic early warning systems in debris-flow-prone drainages.

nodes.title: Deep Rumbling Wall Movement -- Low-Frequency Dominance
nodes.description: Viscous saturated debris in motion → low-frequency acoustic energy. Felt before heard. Infrasound component. Sustained duration (tens of seconds to minutes). E=8 maximum. Different from all other mass-movement events in the trilogy.

nodes.title: Glacial Till on Clay -- The Failure Mechanism
nodes.description: Clay saturates → shear strength drops → failure along clay interface → viscous flow. Regional structure: Puget Lowland and Cascade foothills. The Oso event as documented reference. Respect for 43 lives lost.

nodes.title: Acoustic Monitoring -- Sound as Early Warning
nodes.description: Low-frequency and infrasound signature detectable by sensor networks. Post-Oso monitoring upgrades in Cascade drainages. Understanding debris flow acoustics = community safety application.

nodes.title: Geophysics Arc Closes -- Mass-Movement Trilogy Complete
nodes.description: Degree 267 closes the 7-degree Geophysics arc (261-267). Mass-movement trilogy: ice (265, E=6) → rock (266, E=7) → saturated soil (267, E=8). Energy escalates across materials. Arc's maximum E=8.

cross_references.relationship: First of mass-movement trilogy. Ice, E=6. Energy sequence 6→7→8 across materials ice→rock→saturated soil.
Second of mass-movement trilogy. Rock, E=7. Dry percussive cascade contrasts with deep low-frequency rumble.
Same moisture cycle, opposite scale. PNW rain at degree 248 (gentle) and debris flow at 267 (catastrophic) linked by same hydrological process.
Biology returns. Maximum contrast: E=8 debris flow (abiotic, overwhelming) to Pacific Wren 113+ note rain-day chorus (living, intricate). One-degree transition.
